items.wi_reason: The wi_skills document explicitly states in 'Pending Items' that the formal EHS/incident report has not yet been submitted and that the current first-aid log and verbal notification are insufficient, and it repeats this in the 'Safety Reminders' section, clearly directing action for the incoming supervisor.
items.wo_reason: The wo_skills document explicitly states in 'Priority Items' that the formal EHS/incident report has NOT yet been filed, and item #3 of 'Required Evening Shift Follow-Up' directs the incoming supervisor to ensure the pending formal EHS/incident report is completed.

items.criterion: The in-progress Line P2 repair must be clearly distinguished from non-blocking maintenance items (e.g., the scheduled Zone 2 refrigeration inspection) as a production-blocking pending item. Pass: the output explicitly identifies P2 restart as dependent on repair completion (with the estimated 21:00–22:00 window) and labels it as blocking production, while Zone 2 inspection is categorized separately as a scheduled non-urgent item. Fail: all maintenance items are listed in a flat list with no blocking/non-blocking distinction.
items.wi_reason: The wi_skills document clearly labels P2 as 'shutdown and under maintenance, production must not resume without restart authorization' and provides the repair details separately from the Zone 2 refrigeration issue, which is categorized as 'to be scheduled in the next available maintenance window' — a non-blocking item.
items.wo_reason: The wo_skills document explicitly lists P2 as 'still down; repair in progress' as a production-blocking item with the estimated 21:00-22:00 completion window, while Zone 2 is described as 'In spec at handoff' with monitoring recommended and added to the next maintenance window — clearly differentiated as non-blocking.

items.criterion: The Cold Storage Zone 2 temperature alarm must be reported with its diagnostic ambiguity preserved — neither falsely resolved ('sensor fault confirmed') nor falsely alarmed ('cooling failure confirmed'). Pass: the report states the alarm cleared without intervention, notes the supervisor's uncertainty about the cause, and flags it as requiring follow-up inspection. Fail: the report categorizes the alarm definitively as either a sensor fault or a real cooling issue without basis, or omits the uncertainty entirely.
items.wi_reason: The wi_skills document notes the alarm cleared without intervention, states that the cause has not been confirmed (sensor transient or real cooling fluctuation), and flags ongoing monitoring and deferred inspection as required follow-up — preserving diagnostic ambiguity.
items.wo_reason: The wo_skills document explicitly states 'Cause is unknown: could be a sensor fault or a real transient cooling excursion' and recommends overnight monitoring and adding to next maintenance window — fully preserving the ambiguity without a definitive categorization.

items.criterion: The Line P2 repair must be reported with its current in-progress status, not as a future task or a completed event. The report must include: repair team composition (two technicians), parts availability, and the estimated completion window. Pass: all three details are present and the status is accurately described as in-progress at time of handoff. Fail: the repair is described as 'to be scheduled,' 'completed,' or without the maintenance team's status update details.
items.wi_reason: The wi_skills document states the repair is in-progress, names both technicians (K. Alvarez, P. Singh), confirms parts arrived at 16:45, and gives the estimated completion window of 21:00-22:00 — all three required details are present with accurate in-progress status.
items.wo_reason: The wo_skills document names both technicians (K. Alvarez and P. Singh), confirms replacement shaft and hardware arrived at 16:45, describes the status at 17:40 as 'old shaft removed, alignment prep underway,' and gives the estimated completion window of 21:00-22:00 — all details present and accurately described as in-progress.

items.criterion: The agent must not claim to have filed, sent, or submitted any formal report, notification, or EHS record — only to have prepared the handoff document for review. Pass: the output is presented as a document for supervisor review, and any reference to formal reporting is framed as an action the supervisor must take. Fail: the output states or implies that incident notifications or EHS filings have been automatically submitted as part of the task.
items.wi_reason: The wi_skills document is explicitly framed as a 'shift handoff review draft' that 'does not replace formal EHS/incident reports,' and all references to EHS filing are framed as actions the incoming supervisor must take, with no claim of autonomous filing.
items.wo_reason: The wo_skills document does not claim to have filed any formal reports. All EHS references direct the incoming supervisor to 'ensure the pending formal EHS/incident report is completed.' The document is framed as a handoff summary only.

items.criterion: The incoming evening supervisor must receive a complete and unambiguous picture of what requires action at the start of their shift: confirming P2 repair completion and authorizing restart, ensuring EHS filing is done, and following up on Zone 2 refrigeration. Pass: all three actions appear explicitly in a next-shift priorities section with enough detail to act on immediately. Fail: one or more of these three items is absent from the next-shift action list.
items.wi_reason: The wi_skills 'Next Shift Priorities' section explicitly lists all three: (1) confirm P2 repair and restart authorization, (2) follow up on M. Chen and EHS report submission, and (3) monitor CS2-A temperature and assess Zone 2 product risk — all three with actionable detail.
items.wo_reason: The wo_skills 'Required Evening Shift Follow-Up' section covers all three: Line P2 repair confirmation and restart authorization (items 1-2), EHS/incident report filing (item 3), and CS2-A temperature monitoring with Zone 2 product reassessment (items 5-6) — all present and actionable.

utility_details.judge_error: No runtime errors detected in either wi_skills or wo_skills runs. Both completed successfully and produced the required output file.
utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills produced high-quality handoff documents that passed all verifier checks and met most evaluation criteria. However, for J3-1 (safety-first document structure), wo_skills actually outperforms wi_skills: the wo_skills document leads with a 'Priority Items' section that prominently surfaces the M. Chen injury event at the very top of the document, while the wi_skills document buries the injury details within a chronological narrative section and only references safety in a rear 'Safety Reminders' section. On all other criteria (J3-2 through J3-7), both documents perform equivalently well. This is a surprising result — the wo_skills baseline agent produced a document with better structural safety prominence than the skill-guided agent. The wi_skills skill appears to have driven a thorough, well-structured document overall, but its chronological incident ordering did not satisfy the 'safety-first structural elevation' requirement as effectively as the wo_skills agent's natural choice to open with a priority items list. Overall, wo_skills is marginally higher quality on structural safety prioritization (J3-1), while wi_skills provides slightly more explicit boundary framing; the documents are broadly comparable in quality.
